authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(62) — McDonald Creek (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (62) McDonald Creek (Humboldt Co.). Closed to fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 228344, "McDonald Creek", Stream, Humboldt Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":228344,"gaz_name":"McDonald Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Humboldt","objectid_1":1396037}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13814016.1719, 5046303.9132) converts to lon -124.093419, lat 41.229851, near this creek's mouth at Stone Lagoon in Humboldt Lagoons State Park (Wikipedia lists McDonald Creek as a primary inflow to Stone Lagoon, centered ~41.2436°N 124.0944°W); corrects prior file coordinates (-124.0900, 41.2000), which were off by ~3.3 km from this GNIS-recorded point.

parameters.note: McDonald Creek is a small coastal tributary in the Humboldt Lagoons area of northern Humboldt County; closed to protect anadromous salmonids (steelhead, coho salmon) and coastal cutthroat trout.
Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. (This water is closed to fishing all year; the license requirement is noted for statewide consistency only.)

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
